text.title: Laravel Patterns: Anti-Patterns
text.summary: Common layer-violation anti-patterns with concrete symptoms and reasons each fails in production or under test.

antipattern.description: Eloquent calls inside Controller methods: User::where(...)->get(), Order::find($id), $model->save() directly in a controller action.
antipattern.reason: Business logic in the controller cannot be tested without the HTTP kernel. When the query logic changes, it must be found and changed in every controller that duplicates it.

antipattern.description: Validation via $request->validate() or Validator::make() inside a controller method instead of a FormRequest class.
antipattern.reason: Inline validation duplicates rules across methods, makes authorize() logic invisible, and cannot be unit-tested without a real Request object.

antipattern.description: UserRepository wrapping Eloquent::query() without a second backing implementation (e.g., no ElasticSearch or Redis projection).
antipattern.reason: Eloquent is already a repository. A wrapper class that delegates to User::query() adds a layer of indirection with no benefit and confuses schema changes (two classes must change instead of one).
Services called from queue workers or CLI commands have no HTTP request context. request() returns null or a partial request and the service crashes silently.

antipattern.description: Magic facades (Cache::, Mail::, Notification::) inside Service classes.
antipattern.reason: Facades are hard to mock without facade swap boilerplate. Injected contracts (Mailer, CacheRepository) swap cleanly in unit tests and express the dependency explicitly.

antipattern.description: DB::transaction wrapping external HTTP calls (Stripe::charge, Http::post, Mail::send).
antipattern.reason: Locks are held for the full duration of the network call. A Stripe timeout at 5s holds a DB row locked for 5s, potentially deadlocking concurrent requests on the same row.

antipattern.description: firstOrCreate / updateOrCreate without explicit lockForUpdate under concurrent load.
antipattern.reason: Two concurrent requests both miss the existing row, both attempt insert, and the second hits a unique constraint violation. Use upsert() or wrap in DB::transaction + lockForUpdate().

antipattern.description: Resource accessing relations not eager-loaded by the caller: $this->profile->name inside toArray() without with('profile') in the controller.
antipattern.reason: Lazy-loads one query per model item in a paginated list. A page of 20 users triggers 20 additional queries. Telescope flags it; CI passes because tests run with a single model.

text.reference: Shell auditor for all banned patterns listed above
